mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack reduces corporate profit margins and consumer spending growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of periods where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, which forces earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST coincide with positive SPX returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial production growth, supply-side capacity utilization improves corporate operating leverage, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth periods yield negative 5-day returns in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that crosses above 3.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate when FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.0% in >55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation deterioration, which reduces aggregate demand and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE growth predict positive returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved earnings.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth correlates with negative 5-day returns in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production crosses below its 12-month moving average, manufacturing contraction signals weakening economic momentum, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in >60% of periods where INDPRO crosses below its 12-month MA.
